title: Archer Acquires Hawthorne Airport for Electric Air-Taxi Hub
text: Archer Aviation completed first phase of Hawthorne Airport transactions including acquisition of master lease from City of Hawthorne and associated subleases, giving it control of real estate making up airport. Acquisition was for approximately $126 million in cash. Airport expected to serve as operational hub for planned LA air taxi network operations, including serving key role in LA28 Olympic & Paralympic Games. Hawthorne Airport located in heart of LA, sits on 80-acre site and includes approximately 190,000 square feet of terminal, office and hangar facilities. Historic Hawthorne Airport built in 1920s and once helped shape Southern California's aerospace legacy, also known as Jack Northrop Field. Strategically located less than three miles from LAX and closest airport to city's biggest attractions: SoFi Stadium, The Forum, Intuit Dome and Downtown LA. Archer plans to utilize airport as innovation testbed for next-generation AI-powered aviation technologies developing and planning to deploy with airline and technology partners, including AI-powered air traffic and ground operations management. Plans to acquire airport's FBO and passenger terminal next year in second phase of transaction, giving Archer full control of airport and day-to-day operations.
